discovery_gate.why: Existing video clippers require local GPU/storage/bandwidth. SushiVideo leverages Google Colab's free infrastructure (GPU, fast internet, storage) to process large videos into short clips — making it extremely portable and zero-cost.
discovery_gate.who: Solo content creators who repurpose long YouTube videos into TikTok/Reels shorts, especially those without a powerful local PC.
discovery_gate.core: Full pipeline: Input (YouTube URL via Telegram or large video upload via Gradio, + optional SRT) → AI segment selection → automated FFmpeg editing (portrait reformat, 1.25x speed, hardsub) → finished short clips delivered via Telegram. Output 1 (CSV + SRT) is intermediate validation.
discovery_gate.landscape: Greenfield — new project from scratch. TTB (D:\TTB) serves as reference only for patterns, not copy-paste.
discovery_gate.success: Given a 1-hour YouTube video URL, SushiVideo produces 3-5 portrait short clips with hardsubs and delivers them to Telegram on a Colab GPU.

constraints.runtime: Google Colab (GPU required, no CPU support)
